_comment: Example Claude Code settings hooks for crm-ai-operators. Drop the relevant pieces into your .claude/settings.json (or .claude/settings.local.json). All hooks here are opt-in and reversible.

PostToolUse._purpose: Append every operation_run call to a daily activity log so you can trace what your agent ran without parsing the audit JSONL by hand.
After a setup.apply run, dump a compact summary so you can see what changed in this session.

hooks.command: tail -1 data/audit/$(date +%Y-%m-%d).jsonl | jq -r 'if .meta.operation == "setup.apply" then "setup.apply: " + (.meta.summary // "no summary") else empty end' 2>/dev/null || true

PreToolUse._purpose: Safety gate — refuse to run live (DRY_RUN=false) operations that aren't idempotent unless the user has explicitly authorized in this session. Replace the `agree-to-live-writes` token check with whatever your team uses.

hooks.command: if [ "$DRY_RUN" = "false" ] && [ ! -f .claude/agree-to-live-writes ]; then echo 'BLOCKED: DRY_RUN=false but no .claude/agree-to-live-writes token. Touch the file to authorize this session.' >&2; exit 1; fi

Notification._purpose: Forward any handoff alert (severity=critical, category=handoff) to a Slack webhook so reps see it in <1 minute. Set CRM_AI_SLACK_WEBHOOK in your environment.

hooks.command: if [ -n "$CRM_AI_SLACK_WEBHOOK" ] && echo "$CLAUDE_NOTIFICATION" | grep -q 'handoff'; then curl -s -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' --data "{\"text\":\"$CLAUDE_NOTIFICATION\"}" "$CRM_AI_SLACK_WEBHOOK" >/dev/null; fi

_recommended_workflow: 1. Copy the hooks block into .claude/settings.local.json (project-scoped, gitignored).
2. Test with: PERSONIZE_SECRET_KEY=fake DRY_RUN=true npx tsx src/scripts/crm-agent.ts operation list
3. To enable live writes for a session, run: touch .claude/agree-to-live-writes && DRY_RUN=false ...
4. Remove the token file when you're done: rm .claude/agree-to-live-writes
